text: The patient is positioned in lithotomy, and a topical anesthesia solution is sprayed over the area. A total of five milliliters of the solution is injected into the subcutaneous plane of the perianal region in a circumanal manner, with 2.5 milliliters administered on each side.

The surgeon replaces the needle with a long spinal needle and injects five ml of local anesthesia solution into each ischiorectal fossa, approximately six centimeters deep, targeting the center of the fossa without inserting a finger into the rectum or approaching the pudendal vessels.

The surgeon injects three ml of solution into the posterior pre-sacral space and two ml into the anterior sphincter complex. After waiting for one minute, a lubricated finger is inserted into the rectum, applying slight posterior traction to relax the sphincter complex before commencing the surgery.
